differential_diagnoses: Intracranial hypertension due to electrolyte disturbance or medication effects
next_steps: Emergent MRI/MRV of the brain to evaluate for venous sinus thrombosis and intracranial pathology
Comprehensive coagulation panel including PT/INR, PTT, fibrinogen, and platelet count
Ophthalmologic evaluation with funduscopy to assess for papilledema
Lumbar puncture with opening pressure, cell count, protein, glucose, and meningitis/encephalitis panel if neuroimaging is non-diagnostic
Review and adjustment of medications potentially contributing to intracranial pressure or neurotoxicity
